hero.headline: Kentucky at-will employment and termination law
hero.subhead: At-will with three real exceptions. Final paycheck due the later of next payday or 14 days. KCRA fires at 8 employees, not 15.

secondary_cta.label: Check your EOR vs entity crossover

at_a_glance.final_paycheck_deadline: Later of next regular payday or 14 calendar days
at_a_glance.vacation_payout: Not required by law; governed by employer policy
at_a_glance.state_warn_act: None — federal WARN (100+ employees, 60 days) governs

at_will_rule.question: What is Kentucky's at-will employment rule?
at_will_rule.answer_first: Kentucky is an at-will employment state: either party can end the relationship at any time, for any lawful reason or no reason, with no mandatory notice period. Three exceptions limit that default.

exceptions.question: What are the exceptions to at-will employment in Kentucky?
exceptions.answer_first: Kentucky recognises three exceptions: public policy, implied contract, and statutory anti-retaliation protections. Each has a distinct trigger and a distinct exposure window.

final_paycheck.question: When is the final paycheck due in Kentucky?
final_paycheck.answer_first: Under KRS 337.055, final wages are due the later of the next regular payday or 14 calendar days after separation. Not the sooner — the later.

deadline_table.deadline: Later of: next regular payday or 14 calendar days
Later of: next regular payday or 14 calendar days
Required only if employer policy mandates payout

final_paycheck.penalty: Unpaid wages + equal amount as liquidated damages (KRS 337.385)

kcra_protections.question: How many employees does the Kentucky Civil Rights Act apply to?
kcra_protections.answer_first: The KCRA (KRS Chapter 344) applies at 8 employees for most protected classes and 15 employees for disability and pregnancy, both requiring 20 or more weeks of employment per year.

threshold_table.class: Race, sex, age (40+), religion, national origin

kcra_protections.limitations_period_change: Reduced from 5 years to 3 years effective 15 July 2024 (HB 320)

warn_act.question: Does Kentucky have a state WARN Act?
warn_act.answer_first: No. Kentucky has no state-level plant-closing or mass-layoff notice law. Federal WARN applies to employers with 100 or more employees, requiring 60 days' written notice of a qualifying event.

cobra_continuation.question: What happens to health insurance coverage after termination in Kentucky?
cobra_continuation.answer_first: Federal COBRA applies if you employ 20 or more workers. Kentucky's state continuation law covers employers with fewer than 20 employees, providing up to 18 months of continued group coverage.

coverage_table.duration: 18 months general; 36 months for dependents on second qualifying event

coverage_table.applies_to: Employers with fewer than 20 employees, fully-insured plans

good_to_know.question: What should you know before terminating a Kentucky employee?
good_to_know.answer_first: Three Kentucky-specific facts most out-of-state employers miss: the 'later of' final paycheck rule, the 8-employee KCRA threshold, and the implied-contract risk in unqualified handbook language.

meta.title: Kentucky Termination Law and At-Will Exceptions | Teamed
meta.description: Kentucky is at-will with a 3-exception rule. Final paycheck due the later of next payday or 14 days (KRS 337.055). KCRA fires at 8 employees. EOR via Teamed at $599/month flat, Zero FX.
